req.title: Manager views employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-004 requires a manager to view an employee via the UI at /employees. Three distinct risk areas drive test selection: (1) the happy-path where a valid manager views an existing employee and an audit record is written (EP – valid partition); (2) an unauthenticated access attempt returning 401 (NEG); (3) a boundary condition on the employee identifier field at its minimum valid length of 1 character vs 0 characters (BVA). Additional cases cover forbidden role (DT – role column) and the state transition from 'authenticated' to 'viewing employee' (STC). Together these five cases satisfy the minimum three required while covering all cited error pathways.

tests.name: test_manager_views_existing_employee_success
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
At least one employee record exists in the system
actions: Obtain a valid manager session token via <fixture: manager_auth_token>
Navigate to /employees and select an existing employee
Assert the employee detail view is rendered with HTTP 200
Assert an audit record was written via <helper_fn: get_latest_audit_record>
expected: Audit record references the viewed employee id and actor manager
tests.executable:
def test_manager_views_existing_employee_success(manager_auth_token, existing_employee_id, api_client, audit_helper):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"}
    resp = api_client.get(f"/employees/{existing_employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == existing_employee_id
    assert "first_name" in body
    assert "last_name" in body
    audit = audit_helper.get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=existing_employee_id)
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["action"] == "VIEW"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "manager"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_employee
preconditions: No valid session token is present
At least one employee record exists in the system
actions: Send GET /employees/{id} with no Authorization header
Assert response status is 401
Assert no audit record was created for this attempt
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
No audit record written for the unauthenticated attempt
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_employee(existing_employee_id, api_client, audit_helper):
    before_count = audit_helper.count_audit_records(entity_id=existing_employee_id)
    resp = api_client.get(f"/employees/{existing_employee_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    after_count = audit_helper.count_audit_records(entity_id=existing_employee_id)
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit record should be written on 401"

tests.name: test_employee_id_at_minimum_boundary_length
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
An employee record with the shortest valid identifier (length=1) exists
actions: Obtain a valid manager session token via <fixture: manager_auth_token>
Send GET /employees/{id} where id is the single-character minimum identifier
Assert response status is 200 and entity id matches
Send GET /employees/{id} where id is an empty string (length=0)
Assert response status is 400 or 404
expected: Single-character id returns 200 with valid employee body
Empty-string id returns 400 or 404 indicating invalid identifier
trace: R-WEB-004: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
def test_employee_id_at_minimum_boundary_length(manager_auth_token, api_client, min_length_employee_id):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"}
    resp_valid = api_client.get(f"/employees/{min_length_employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp_valid.status_code == 200
    body = resp_valid.json()
    assert str(body["id"]) == str(min_length_employee_id)
    resp_empty = api_client.get("/employees/", headers=headers)
    assert resp_empty.status_code in (400, 404)

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_employee
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds the 'employee' role (not manager)
At least one employee record exists in the system
actions: Obtain a session token for a non-manager user via <fixture: employee_auth_token>
Send GET /employees/{id} with that token
Assert response status is 403
Assert no audit record was written for this attempt
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_employee(employee_auth_token, existing_employee_id, api_client, audit_helper):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_auth_token}"}
    before_count = audit_helper.count_audit_records(entity_id=existing_employee_id)
    resp = api_client.get(f"/employees/{existing_employee_id}", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    after_count = audit_helper.count_audit_records(entity_id=existing_employee_id)
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit record should be written on 403"

tests.name: test_manager_view_employee_state_transition_from_list_to_detail
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
At least one employee record exists and is visible in the list view
actions: Obtain a valid manager session token via <fixture: manager_auth_token>
GET /employees to enter the 'list' state and assert 200
Select the first employee id from the list
GET /employees/{id} to transition to the 'detail' state and assert 200
Assert the detail response body contains the same id as selected from the list
Assert audit record written for the detail view transition
expected: List endpoint returns 200 with a non-empty array
Detail endpoint returns 200 with correct employee entity
State successfully transitions from list to detail
Audit record written on detail view
tests.executable:
def test_manager_view_employee_state_transition_from_list_to_detail(manager_auth_token, api_client, audit_helper):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_token}"}
    list_resp = api_client.get("/employees", headers=headers)
    assert list_resp.status_code == 200
    employees = list_resp.json()
    assert isinstance(employees, list) and len(employees) > 0
    target_id = employees[0]["id"]
    detail_resp = api_client.get(f"/employees/{target_id}", headers=headers)
    assert detail_resp.status_code == 200
    detail_body = detail_resp.json()
    assert detail_body["id"] == target_id
    audit = audit_helper.get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=target_id)
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["action"] == "VIEW"
